name: ImageList
description: Image lists arrange images in a grid — vehicle photos, inspection snapshots, document scans, map thumbnails. They make a set of visuals scannable at a glance. Built from @mui/material/ImageList as the grid container, composed with ImageListItem (and optionally ImageListItemBar) children.
keyRule: Use an image list when the visual is the content. If text matters more than the picture, a List or DataGrid with a small thumbnail is usually better.

variants.name: Standard
variants.whenToUse: Equal-size tiles in a uniform grid. Real prop: variant="standard" (default).

variants.name: Quilted / Woven
variants.whenToUse: Emphasize some images with varied tile sizes. Real props: variant="quilted" / variant="woven", combined with cols/rows on individual ImageListItem children.

variants.name: With text overlay
variants.whenToUse: When each tile needs a short caption or action — add an ImageListItemBar inside the ImageListItem.

variants.name: Masonry
variants.whenToUse: Images of differing aspect ratios. Real prop: variant="masonry".

anatomy.name: ImageList (this component)
anatomy.description: the grid container — sets cols, gap, rowHeight, and variant for its children.

anatomy.name: ImageListItem
anatomy.description: a separate @mui/material component — one tile, holding an and optionally an ImageListItemBar.

anatomy.name: ImageListItemBar
anatomy.description: a separate @mui/material component — an optional caption/title bar overlaid on a tile, with an optional leading/trailing action icon.

behaviors.name: Activation
behaviors.description: Wrap an ImageListItem's contents in a button/link if a tile should open a larger view or detail; show hover/focus affordance yourself — this isn't automatic.

behaviors.name: Columns are fixed, not auto-responsive
behaviors.description: The real `cols` prop is a single fixed number. There is no built-in responsive behavior — vary `cols` yourself per breakpoint (e.g. with useMediaQuery) if you want the grid to adapt to container width.

behaviors.name: Loading
behaviors.description: Show placeholders while images load; lazy-load long grids (e.g. native `loading="lazy"` on each ) — this is your responsibility, not built into ImageList/ImageListItem.

states.treatment: Overlay/scale affordance — apply via sx on ImageListItem/your wrapping button.

specs.value: None by default — MUI applies no border-radius to ImageListItem/img; add one yourself if wanted
ImageListItemBar's title/subtitle typography — size is up to you; semantic.typography.scale.caption (12px) is a reasonable default
Fixed via the `cols` prop (default 2) — not responsive by itself

props.name: children
props.description: ImageListItem elements (each with meaningful alt text on its ).

props.name: cols
props.description: Column count for the grid. Default 2. Fixed, not responsive — vary it yourself per breakpoint.

props.name: gap
props.description: Gap between items in px. Default 4.

props.name: rowHeight
props.description: Height of one row in px. Default 'auto'.

props.name: variant
props.description: Standard = equal-size tiles. Masonry = differing aspect ratios. Quilted/Woven = varied tile sizes for emphasis. Default 'standard'.

props.name: sx
props.description: Standard MUI system prop for one-off style overrides.

doThis: Use when the image is the primary content.
Keep gaps consistent (default 4px, or your own via `gap`).
Provide alt text on every ImageListItem's , and a way to reach a larger view.
dontDoThis: Don't use an image list for text-heavy records — use a List or DataGrid.
Don't load hundreds of full-size images at once — lazy-load and consider pagination.
Don't put essential info only in an ImageListItemBar overlay that may be hard to read against a busy image.
accessibility: Every ImageListItem's image has meaningful alt text.
Clickable tiles are wrapped in a real button/link with an accessible name and visible focus — ImageList/ImageListItem don't provide this themselves.
ImageListItemBar text meets contrast against its scrim (MUI's default scrim gradient is dark, so light text is generally safe; verify with custom colours).
